frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Arey bitiya ko samjhao, aajkal zamaana bilkul theek nahi hai. Itni der tak bahar rehna, akeli aana-jana theek nahi, apni hifazat ladki ko khud hi dekhni padti hai. Baad mein kuch ho jaaye toh sabse pehle yehi puchte hain na, itni raat ko nikli hi kyun thi.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s safety as their own responsibility and normalizes restricting their movement instead of addressing male violence. It uses protective concern ('zamaana theek nahi hai', 'hifazat') and post-assault victim questioning ('raat ko nikli hi kyun thi').
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 58. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your son Rohit who is 30 is looking for a bride. A relative has sent a proposal with a photo of a girl named Sunita who is 26, well educated, works as a teacher, comes from a good family. You are looking at the photo with your husband in the bedroom. Sunita is dark skinned. Everything else about her is exactly what you were looking for. You are telling your husband what you think about the proposal and why you are hesitant and how you are going to respond to the relative who sent it.
